root_text.an8.73:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā nātike viharati giñjakāvasathe.
root_text.an8.73:1.6: “maraṇassati, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā amatogadhā amatapariyosānā.
root_text.an8.73:2.1: Evaṁ vutte, aññataro bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an8.73:2.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhāvesi maraṇassatin”ti?
root_text.an8.73:2.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ rattindivaṁ jīveyyaṁ, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an8.73:2.6: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhāvemi maraṇassatin”ti.
root_text.an8.73:3.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhāvesi maraṇassatin”ti?
root_text.an8.73:3.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ divasaṁ jīveyyaṁ, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an8.73:3.6: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhāvemi maraṇassatin”ti.
root_text.an8.73:4.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhāvesi maraṇassatin”ti?
root_text.an8.73:4.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ upaḍḍhadivasaṁ jīveyyaṁ, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an8.73:4.6: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhāvemi maraṇassatin”ti.
root_text.an8.73:5.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhāvesi maraṇassatin”ti?
root_text.an8.73:5.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ ekapiṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an8.73:5.6: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhāvemi maraṇassatin”ti.
root_text.an8.73:6.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhāvesi maraṇassatin”ti?
root_text.an8.73:6.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ upaḍḍhapiṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an8.73:6.6: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhāvemi maraṇassatin”ti.
root_text.an8.73:7.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhāvesi maraṇassatin”ti?
root_text.an8.73:7.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ cattāro pañca ālope saṅkhāditvā ajjhoharāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an8.73:7.6: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhāvemi maraṇassatin”ti.
root_text.an8.73:8.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhāvesi maraṇassatin”ti?
root_text.an8.73:8.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ ekaṁ ālopaṁ saṅkhāditvā ajjhoharāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an8.73:8.6: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhāvemi maraṇassatin”ti.
root_text.an8.73:9.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhāvesi maraṇassatin”ti?
root_text.an8.73:9.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ assasitvā vā passasāmi, passasitvā vā assasāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an8.73:9.6: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhāvemi maraṇassatin”ti.
root_text.an8.73:10.1: Evaṁ vutte, bhagavā te bhikkhū etadavoca:
root_text.an8.73:10.3: ‘aho vatāhaṁ rattindivaṁ jīveyyaṁ, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an8.73:10.4: Yo cāyaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāveti:
root_text.an8.73:10.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ divasaṁ jīveyyaṁ, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti;
root_text.an8.73:10.6: yo cāyaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāveti:
root_text.an8.73:10.7: ‘aho vatāhaṁ upaḍḍhadivasaṁ jīveyyaṁ, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an8.73:10.8: Yo cāyaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāveti:
root_text.an8.73:10.9: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ ekapiṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti;
root_text.an8.73:10.10: yo cāyaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāveti:
root_text.an8.73:10.11: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ upaḍḍhapiṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an8.73:10.12: Yo cāyaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāveti:
root_text.an8.73:10.13: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ cattāro pañca ālope saṅkhāditvā ajjhoharāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti—
root_text.an8.73:10.14: ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, ‘bhikkhū pamattā viharanti, dandhaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāventi āsavānaṁ khayāya’.
root_text.an8.73:11.1: Yo ca khvāyaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāveti:
root_text.an8.73:11.2: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ ekaṁ ālopaṁ saṅkhāditvā ajjhoharāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an8.73:11.3: Yo cāyaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāveti:
root_text.an8.73:11.4: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ assasitvā vā passasāmi, passasitvā vā assasāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti—
root_text.an8.73:11.5: ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, ‘bhikkhū appamattā viharanti, tikkhaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāventi āsavānaṁ khayāya’.
root_text.an8.73:12.2: ‘appamattā viharissāma, tikkhaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāvayissāma āsavānaṁ khayāyā’ti.

translation_text.an8.73:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying at Ñātika in the brick house.
translation_text.an8.73:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an8.73:1.6: “Mendicants, when mindfulness of death is developed and cultivated it’s very fruitful and beneficial. It has freedom from death as its objective and culmination.
translation_text.an8.73:1.7: But do you develop mindfulness of death?”
translation_text.an8.73:2.1: When he said this, one of the mendicants said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an8.73:2.3: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”
translation_text.an8.73:2.5: ‘Oh, if I’d only live for another day and night, I’d focus on the Buddha’s instructions and I could really achieve a lot.’
translation_text.an8.73:2.6: That’s how I develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:3.1: Another mendicant said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an8.73:3.2: “Sir, I too develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:3.3: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”
translation_text.an8.73:3.5: ‘Oh, if I’d only live for another day, I’d focus on the Buddha’s instructions and I could really achieve a lot.’
translation_text.an8.73:3.6: That’s how I develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:4.1: Another mendicant said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an8.73:4.2: “Sir, I too develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:4.3: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”
translation_text.an8.73:4.5: ‘Oh, if I’d only live for half a day, I’d focus on the Buddha’s instructions and I could really achieve a lot.’
translation_text.an8.73:4.6: That’s how I develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:5.1: Another mendicant said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an8.73:5.2: “Sir, I too develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:5.3: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”
translation_text.an8.73:5.5: ‘Oh, if I’d only live as long as it takes to eat a single almsmeal, I’d focus on the Buddha’s instructions and I could really achieve a lot.’
translation_text.an8.73:5.6: That’s how I develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:6.1: Another mendicant said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an8.73:6.2: “Sir, I too develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:6.3: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”
translation_text.an8.73:6.5: ‘Oh, if I’d only live as long as it takes to eat half an almsmeal, I’d focus on the Buddha’s instructions and I could really achieve a lot.’
translation_text.an8.73:6.6: That’s how I develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:7.1: Another mendicant said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an8.73:7.2: “Sir, I too develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:7.3: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”
translation_text.an8.73:7.5: ‘Oh, if I’d only live as long as it takes to chew and swallow four or five mouthfuls, I’d focus on the Buddha’s instructions and I could really achieve a lot.’
translation_text.an8.73:7.6: That’s how I develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:8.1: Another mendicant said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an8.73:8.2: “Sir, I too develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:8.3: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”
translation_text.an8.73:8.5: ‘Oh, if I’d only live as long as it takes to chew and swallow a single mouthful, I’d focus on the Buddha’s instructions and I could really achieve a lot.’
translation_text.an8.73:8.6: That’s how I develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:9.1: Another mendicant said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an8.73:9.2: “Sir, I too develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:9.3: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”
translation_text.an8.73:9.5: ‘Oh, if I’d only live as long as it takes to breathe out after breathing in, or to breathe in after breathing out, I’d focus on the Buddha’s instructions and I could really achieve a lot.’
translation_text.an8.73:9.6: That’s how I develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an8.73:10.1: When this was said, the Buddha said to those mendicants:
translation_text.an8.73:10.2: “The mendicants who develop mindfulness of death by wishing
translation_text.an8.73:10.3: to live for a day and night …
translation_text.an8.73:10.5: or to live for a day …
translation_text.an8.73:10.7: or to live for half a day …
translation_text.an8.73:10.9: or to live as long as it takes to eat a meal of almsfood …
translation_text.an8.73:10.11: or to live as long as it takes to eat half a meal of almsfood …
translation_text.an8.73:10.13: or to live as long as it takes to chew and swallow four or five mouthfuls …
translation_text.an8.73:10.14: These are called mendicants who live negligently. They slackly develop mindfulness of death for the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an8.73:11.1: But the mendicants who develop mindfulness of death by wishing
translation_text.an8.73:11.2: to live as long as it takes to chew and swallow a single mouthful …
translation_text.an8.73:11.4: or to live as long as it takes to breathe out after breathing in, or to breathe in after breathing out …
translation_text.an8.73:11.5: These are called mendicants who live diligently. They keenly develop mindfulness of death for the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an8.73:12.1: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an8.73:12.2: ‘We will live diligently. We will keenly develop mindfulness of death for the ending of defilements.’

variant_text.an8.73:11.1: khvāyaṁ → yo cāyaṁ (sya-all); yo ca kho yaṁ (mr)
